rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO GONZALEZ VS. DWAIN ALAN DAVIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Lift Stay As To Defendant Dwain Alan Davis
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday March 13, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF FERNANDO GONZALEZ' Motion To Lift Stay As To Defendant Dwain Alan Davis. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Alternatively, the parties may stipulate by email sent to Contestdept302tr@sftc.org and sbs@knoxricksen.com consenting to the authority of the Judge Pro Tem to transfer the motion to the attention of Hon. Harold Kahn whose Order Staying Discovery, dated March 9, 2012, is requested to be lifted. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVIS RAWLINGS VS. TALK FREE, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Re Petitioner David Rawlings Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandamus And Request For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandamus Ordering Inspection Of Corporate Books And Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 13, 2015, Line 2, PETITIONER DAVID RAWLINGS' Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandamus And Request For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandamus Ordering Inspection Of Corporate Books And Records. Denied as moot. The evidence establishes that Respondent has substantially complied with Petitioner's request for documents. Petitioner's request for attorney's fees and costs is denied. The evidence shows that the parties were in the process of meeting and conferring about producing the documents at the time the Petition was filed. As such, Petitioner has not established that the Petition was the catalyst for the production of the records. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHEZ FAYALA, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. KAMEL BOUZIDI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 13, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANTS KAMEL BOUZIDI, AND ADAM AND SARA LLC DBA CAFE MED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication is denied. The court denies summary adjudication as to issues A-E, H, J, and L. Kamel's undisputed fact 7 states as a factual matter that Kamel signed the noncompete agreement on 5/16/13 and undisputed fact 8 states that he executed the purchase contract on 2/12/13. Jamel and Sofiene cite Kamel's responses to request for admission 13 and 17 where he denies signing the agreements and contends that they are forgeries. The cited evidence thus creates a disputed issue of material fact. In the reply, Kamel argues that he is simply assuming for the sake of this motion that the contracts are viable. Kamel, however, did not draft the separate statement to reflect that assertion. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue F. Kamel is correct that there can be no tort recovery on this claim because this is not an insurance case. Kamel, however, failed to maintain his initial burden of production. Plaintiffs' evidence indicates that there is other conduct that does not parrot the breach of express contract cause of action that forms the basis for the claim. The court denies summary adjudication as to issues G, I, and K. Kamel argues that plaintiff's discovery responses are factually devoid and therefore, plaintiff cannot establish the interference claims. That argument fails because Kamel mischaracterizes the responses. While some of the responses could list more facts, several of them are factually rich. For example, response to special interrogatory 24 expressly cites the e-mails that support the claims. The court denies summary adjudication as to issue M. Kamel failed to maintain his initial burden of production. Kamel argues that plaintiffs cannot establish anti-competitive conduct, but he does not address all of plaintiff's claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID RAWLINGS ET AL VS. TALK FREE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Require Plaintiffs To Post Bond In The Amount Of $50,00; Application To Extend 30-Day Period To File Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 13, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANTS DOUG COLE, JOI SCHEELE, STAN TEETS, AND TALK FREE, INC.'S Motion To Require Plaintiffs To Post Bond In The Amount Of $50,00; Application To Extend 30-Day Period To File Motion. Denied. Talk Free, Inc. is a Delaware corporation. Pursuant to the Internal Affairs Doctrine, this dispute is governed by Delaware law. (See Cal. Corp. Code sec. 2116; see also Vaughn v. LJ Intern, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 213, 223.) The requirement of a plaintiff posting a bond in a derivative suit is substantive, not merely procedural. (Koster v. Warren (9th Cir. 1961) 297 F.2d 418, 419-20.) Talk Free, Inc. has not cited a Delaware statute that provides for a plaintiff in a derivative suit posting a bond. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JESSE ANDERSON VS. SAFEWAY, INC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 13, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT G4S SECURE SOLUTIONS (USA) INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The court overrules defendant's dilatory demurrer. The court liberally reads the complaint per CCP 452 and reads the complaint as a whole. The pleading clearly describes facts regarding the nature of the attack and even provides the name of the assailant. Defendant's arguments about vicarious liability and/or ratification raise factual issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer.
